title: Amoora janowskyi
taxonomy: Amoora janowskyi is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cashew family or the sumac family. This family includes around 83 genera and 700 species of plants. Amoora janowskyi specifically belongs to the genus Amoora, which is a genus of deciduous trees native to Southeast Asia and Australia. The genus Amoora is further classified under the order Sapindales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. These classifications are based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Amoora janowskyi is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. The medicinal properties of Amoora janowskyi are attributed to its unique combination of bioactive compounds and alkaloids.
